title: The Canterbury Tales (Ellesmere/Furnivall representation)
summary: The General Corpus artifact is CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/agz8232.xml, a pinned copy of University of Michigan item AGZ8232.0001.001. U-M created the uncorrected OCR/Level-1 encoding in 2006 from Frederick J. Furnivall's Chaucer Society one-manuscript edition, issued in eight parts from 1868 to 1884. Furnivall transcribed the Canterbury Tales core principally from Huntington Library MS EL 26 C 9, while explicitly sourcing appendix texts from British Library Royal 18 C II, Bodleian Arch. Selden B.14, and Cambridge Dd.4.24 and reproducing drawings from Cambridge Gg.4.27. Official Huntington, BSB, and CUDL IIIF presentations, U-M page images, and audited Internet Archive/Google and Wikimedia Commons scans are modeled as distinct carriers with carrier-specific coverage and rights; cached GND and U.S. Copyright Office records support, but do not contain, the target work.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is one composite work representation, not 846 page-image works and not an undifferentiated physical-plus-digital object.

Exactly twenty-four tales are counted in metadata. The Gamelyn appendix, supplementary links, rejected Clerk link, Hooper woodcuts, manuscript-miniature facsimiles, Gg.4.27 drawing appendix, and 1907 Flügel correction leaf remain visible as non-counted apparatus.

The Internet Archive/Google carrier is a discontiguous partial scan: it ends the main text during the Second Nun's Tale, then supplies Gamelyn and publisher matter; it nevertheless includes all twenty-three Hooper cuts and the Flügel leaf but no separate twenty-three-miniature facsimile suite.

The Wikimedia Commons DLI carrier is also partial relative to the complete edition: it contains the full main text through the Parson's Tale and Retraction, Gamelyn, the rejected Clerk link, all twenty-three Hooper cuts, and Parson analysis, but omits appendix pp. 27*-28*, the Gg.4.27 drawings, the twenty-three miniature facsimiles, and the Flügel leaf.

The U-M current No Copyright-United States statement, its embedded 2006 restrictions notice, Google scan-use notice, BSB NoC-NC statements, Huntington policy, CUDL's separate image and metadata licenses, public-domain status of the nineteenth-century edition, physical-object non-applicability, and the CME repository's software-worded Unlicense are scoped separately rather than merged.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for agz8232
entities.description: The exact pinned repository XML selected for work ID agz8232.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AGZ8232.0001.001
entities.description: U-M's uncorrected OCR text and Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries-style encoding, linked to an 846-page image sequence. Its header states that no OCR corrections or content editing were performed.

entities.label: Furnivall's one-manuscript Ellesmere edition, 1868-1884
alternate_labels: The Ellesmere MS. of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
entities.description: The immediate printed source for the U-M encoding: a Chaucer Society diplomatic-style print of the Ellesmere text with separately sourced appendices, twenty-three Hooper woodcut teller drawings, twenty-three manuscript-miniature facsimiles, and a 1907 correction leaf. It was issued in eight parts and later bound in varying carrier configurations.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Ellesmere MS. of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, ed. Frederick J. Furnivall, Chaucer Society, First Series nos. 2, 8, 16, 26, 32, 38, 50, 70 (London: N. Trübner, 1868-1884; final title page 1868-1879), x, [3], 683 [i.e. 687], 28* pp., 3 leaves, 46 plates; Flügel correction leaf, 1907.
bibliographic.title: The Ellesmere MS. of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales

entities.label: Huntington Library MS EL 26 C 9 (Ellesmere Chaucer)
entities.description: Deluxe illuminated manuscript of The Canterbury Tales, produced in England c. 1400-1410. It is the principal manuscript source of Furnivall's edition and contains twenty-three teller miniatures, including a likely portrait of Chaucer.

holding.notes: The current digital item records 1995 conservation and directs use through the Huntington Digital Library.

physical_description.extent: ii modern + iv contemporary + 232 main folios + iv modern + ii contemporary
notes: The official digital record catalogs the manuscript c. 1400-1410 and describes twenty-two pilgrim miniatures plus a likely Chaucer portrait.

entities.label: Huntington 480-canvas IIIF presentation of the Ellesmere Chaucer
entities.description: The official provider-published sequence: canvases 1-8 are preliminary leaves, 9-472 reproduce fols. 1r-232v, and 473-480 are final leaves. The 480-canvas sequence is complete as the published IIIF inventory but omits four of the twelve non-text leaves listed in the physical description.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images generated from 800-DPI scans of post-conservation color transparencies

entities.label: University of Michigan 846-page image sequence for AGZ8232.0001.001
entities.description: The U-M page-image layer linked from the OCR encoding. XML PB milestones enumerate 846 unique page images in exact sequence 1-846; the full plate section is represented by PB 747-841 under the omitted Ellesmere-portraits division.
entities.reproduction_format: U-M Digital Collections page-image viewer; source header describes 846 600-DPI TIFF G4 page images

entities.label: Internet Archive/Google scan ellesmeremschau00chaugoog
entities.description: A 667-page discontiguous partial carrier. It includes the main text through Second Nun line 413 at printed p. 538, Gamelyn pp. 1*-26*, all twenty-three Hooper cuts at PDF pages 202-228 even and 294-310 even, the Flügel correction leaf at PDF page 20, and publisher matter. It omits the remainder of the Second Nun, Canon's Yeoman, Manciple, Parson, Retraction, appendix pp. 27*-28*, Gg.4.27 drawings, and the twenty-three manuscript-miniature facsimiles.
entities.reproduction_format: Google Books scan distributed by Internet Archive as original and text-layer PDFs

entities.label: Internet Archive OCR for ellesmeremschau00chaugoog
entities.description: Internet Archive full-text derivative of the partial Google scan; it cannot supply portions absent from that carrier.

entities.label: Internet Archive metadata for ellesmeremschau00chaugoog
entities.description: Machine-readable item metadata, MARC, and scandata for the partial Google carrier.

entities.label: Wikimedia Commons DLI Granth 106881 PDF
entities.description: A 782-page partial historical-edition carrier despite its Part I file title. It contains the complete main printed text through pp. 1-681, the Parson analysis, Retraction, Gamelyn pp. 1*-26*, rejected Clerk link p. 275*, and all twenty-three Hooper cuts. It omits pp. 27*-28*, the Gg.4.27 drawing appendix, twenty-three manuscript-miniature facsimiles, and the Flügel correction leaf.

entities.label: BSB digital facsimile of Furnivall Part VI (bsb11319145)
entities.description: Official 54-canvas BSB IIIF presentation of Part VI, cataloged as printed pp. 539-586 and dated 1875.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and page images

entities.label: BSB digital facsimile of Furnivall Part VII (bsb11359399)
entities.description: Official 104-canvas BSB IIIF presentation of Part VII, cataloged as printed pp. 589-681 and dated 1877. Canvases 3-8, provider labels 587-592, are the six-page analytical contents of the Parson's Tale; main Parson text begins at canvas 9.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and page images

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 C II
entities.description: Manuscript source identified by Furnivall for the two-line Gamelyn link and Tale of Gamelyn, at fols. 56v-67v.

holding.notes: The official catalog supplies a physical request route; digital images are not available from the catalog item.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Arch. Selden B.14
entities.description: Manuscript source named by Furnivall for the twenty-eight-line Man of Law-Shipman link printed at p. 27*.

holding.notes: No exact public item page or digital facsimile was verified during this review.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.4.24
entities.description: Manuscript source of the sixteen-line Nun's Priest end-link. Furnivall's fol. 195b corresponds to the current CUDL label 195v, canvas 348 of 377.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.27
entities.description: Manuscript source for Furnivall's appendix drawings of the Cook, Reeve, Wife, Pardoner, Monk, and Manciple and six allegorical figures.

holding.notes: CUDL publishes a complete 1,144-canvas facsimile of manuscript part Gg.4.27(1).

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library IIIF facsimile of MS Dd.4.24
entities.description: The complete provider-published 377-canvas CUDL sequence. The sixteen-line Nun's Priest end-link used by Furnivall is on current folio label 195v, canvas 348.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG page images

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library IIIF facsimile of MS Gg.4.27(1)
entities.description: The complete provider-published 1,144-canvas CUDL sequence. Furnivall's appendix drawings occur at nine discontiguous loci spanning canvases 383-877.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG page images

entities.label: GND authority record 117540579 for Frederick James Furnivall
entities.description: Official differentiated-person authority record naming Frederick James Furnivall, born 4 February 1825 and died 2 July 1910, with philologist and OED-founder context. The preferred name and full dates reconcile the editor named on the edition title page despite conflicting legacy VIAF routes.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A (April 2026)
entities.description: The official current Circular 15A used for the United States copyright-duration conclusion, cached separately from the historical source objects it helps evaluate.

agents.name: Frederick James Furnivall
notes: The cached GND record is the controlling term evidence: preferred name Frederick James Furnivall, 1825-02-04 to 1910-07-02. It links a different legacy VIAF URI (71467450) than the previously recorded VIAF route; the title-page name, exact dates, and GND identity establish the intended editor without treating the VIAF mismatch as term evidence.

agents.name: W. H. Hooper
notes: Credited with cutting the twenty-three teller drawings on wood.

agents.name: Ewald Flügel
notes: Credited on the correction leaf dated 1907.

rights.component: Repository XML, OCR text, and markup as checked in
rights.basis: The CME root LICENSE expressly dedicates software to the public domain but does not clearly license XML/data content. The medieval text and nineteenth-century edition are separately public domain; that does not resolve ownership or contractual status of the 2006 OCR and markup.
notes: Do not extend the software-worded Unlicense to source XML by implication.

rights.component: U-M digital text and encoding
rights.basis: The current U-M item states that the materials are in the public domain and assigns No Copyright-United States.
notes: The embedded 2006 header instead says copyright may apply and other distribution restrictions may apply. The newer item-level statement is recorded as current, but the conflict is preserved rather than silently erased.

rights.component: Published medieval text, Furnivall editorial matter, Hooper woodcuts, miniature plates, typography, appendices, and 1907 Flügel corrections
rights.basis: The edition and all identified components were published no later than 1907, well before the U.S. pre-1931 public-domain cutoff in force in 2026.

rights.component: Furnivall's published edition text and expired typographical arrangement
rights.basis: The cached official GND authority record identifies the editor as Frederick James Furnivall and gives his death date as 2 July 1910, so life-plus-70 copyright in his published contribution expired; the edition's typographical arrangement also exceeds the United Kingdom's 25-year term. This assertion is limited to the published edition and does not decide rights in otherwise unpublished manuscript variants.

rights.component: Exact Ellesmere Canterbury Tales text and twenty-three teller miniatures published in Furnivall's edition
rights.jurisdiction: United States and United Kingdom historical-work analysis
rights.basis: These exact medieval textual and artistic components were demonstrably published in Furnivall's edition no later than 1907; the edition is public domain under the cited United States publication rule and United Kingdom term analysis.
notes: This does not assert a blanket United Kingdom status for any Ellesmere content or variants not shown to have been published before 1989; such material is outside this record and may require separate analysis of the possible 2039 term.

rights.component: Physical Ellesmere codex as a material object
rights.jurisdiction: Copyright layer; physical property and reading-room terms are separate
rights.basis: Copyright status does not attach to ownership of the physical manuscript object. Huntington's custody, reading-room rules, handling requirements, and reproduction-service terms are separate access and property layers.

rights.component: Huntington IIIF manifest and digital page images
rights.basis: The item-specific manifest supplies no open license and links only to Huntington's general Library Rights and Permissions policy. That policy does not require permission or a fee for public-domain material or material whose copyright Huntington does not own, asks for citation, and leaves rights research to the user, but it does not expressly dedicate this image set.
notes: Public viewing and a research cache do not by themselves establish a redistribution license for the exact files.

rights.component: U-M digital page images
rights.basis: The current U-M item applies a public-domain/No Copyright-United States statement to the digital item.
notes: The embedded 2006 restriction language is retained as a conflicting historical notice.

rights.component: Google-scanned PDF carrier of a public-domain edition
rights.basis: The underlying 1868-1907 edition content is public domain in the United States. The file's Google notice nevertheless requests attribution and limits file use to personal non-commercial purposes; those are recorded as provider terms rather than treated as new copyright in the historical edition.

rights.component: Machine OCR derivative
rights.basis: The OCR mechanically represents a public-domain historical edition; no separate copyright assertion for the plain-text derivative was found. Google/Internet Archive file-use terms may still apply to the service copy.

rights.component: Internet Archive item metadata, MARC, and scandata files
rights.basis: No file-specific open license for all machine-readable catalog derivatives was verified; factual metadata and provider file terms are distinct questions.

rights.component: Exact Wikimedia Commons PDF derivative
rights.basis: The Commons file page identifies the historical scan as public domain; the underlying edition is independently public domain in the United States.

rights.component: BSB Part VI digital facsimile and IIIF object
rights.basis: The exact BSB IIIF manifest assigns RightsStatements.org No Copyright-Non-Commercial Use Only.
notes: NoC-NC is not an open license and its noncommercial restriction attaches to this provider derivative, not to the underlying public-domain edition wording.

rights.component: BSB Part VII digital facsimile and IIIF object
rights.basis: The exact BSB IIIF manifest assigns RightsStatements.org No Copyright-Non-Commercial Use Only.
notes: NoC-NC is not an open license and its noncommercial restriction attaches to this provider derivative, not to the underlying public-domain edition wording.

rights.component: Exact two-line Gamelyn link and Tale of Gamelyn published in Furnivall's appendix
rights.jurisdiction: United States and United Kingdom historical-work analysis
rights.basis: These exact medieval passages were demonstrably published in Furnivall's edition by 1884; the cited United States publication cutoff and United Kingdom edition-term analysis therefore apply to the published passages.
notes: No blanket conclusion is made for other Royal 18 C II text or unpublished variants; those are outside this record and may require separate United Kingdom 2039-term analysis.

rights.component: Physical Royal MS 18 C II as a material object
rights.jurisdiction: Copyright layer; physical property and reading-room terms are separate
rights.basis: Copyright status does not attach to ownership of the physical manuscript object. British Library request, handling, and reading-room conditions are separate access and property layers.
notes: The catalog's CC BY notice governs catalog-description content, not the physical codex or a nonexistent public image set.

rights.component: Exact twenty-eight-line Man of Law-Shipman link published at Furnivall appendix p. 27*
rights.jurisdiction: United States and United Kingdom historical-work analysis
rights.basis: This exact medieval passage was demonstrably published in Furnivall's edition by 1884; the cited United States publication cutoff and United Kingdom edition-term analysis therefore apply to the published passage.
notes: No blanket conclusion is made for other Arch. Selden B.14 text or unpublished variants; those are outside this record and may require separate United Kingdom 2039-term analysis.

rights.component: Physical MS Arch. Selden B.14 as a material object
rights.jurisdiction: Copyright layer; physical property and reading-room terms are separate
rights.basis: Copyright status does not attach to ownership of the physical manuscript object. Current custody, request, handling, and reading-room terms must be established separately.

rights.component: Exact sixteen-line Nun's Priest end-link published at Furnivall appendix p. 28*
rights.jurisdiction: United States and United Kingdom historical-work analysis
rights.basis: This exact medieval passage was demonstrably published in Furnivall's edition by 1884; the cited United States publication cutoff and United Kingdom edition-term analysis therefore apply to the published passage.
notes: No blanket conclusion is made for other Dd.4.24 text or unpublished variants; those are outside this record and may require separate United Kingdom 2039-term analysis.

rights.component: Physical MS Dd.4.24 as a material object
rights.jurisdiction: Copyright layer; physical property and reading-room terms are separate
rights.basis: Copyright status does not attach to ownership of the physical manuscript object. Cambridge custody, reading-room, and handling terms are separate access and property layers.

rights.component: Exact twelve manuscript drawings published in Furnivall's appendix
rights.jurisdiction: United States and United Kingdom historical-work analysis
rights.basis: These exact medieval drawings were demonstrably published in Furnivall's edition by 1884; the cited United States publication cutoff and United Kingdom edition-term analysis therefore apply to those published drawings.
notes: No blanket conclusion is made for other Gg.4.27 text, artwork, or unpublished variants; those are outside this record and may require separate United Kingdom 2039-term analysis.

rights.component: Physical MS Gg.4.27 as a material object
rights.jurisdiction: Copyright layer; physical property and reading-room terms are separate
rights.basis: Copyright status does not attach to ownership of the physical manuscript object. Cambridge custody, reading-room, and handling terms are separate access and property layers.

rights.component: Downloadable CUDL page images in the MS Dd.4.24 IIIF bundle
rights.basis: The exact CUDL manifest says downloadable images are licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0; this file license is distinct from copyright in the published appendix passage and from ownership of the physical manuscript.

rights.component: CUDL IIIF manifest and descriptive metadata for MS Dd.4.24
rights.basis: The exact CUDL manifest separately licenses metadata under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0.

rights.component: Downloadable CUDL page images in the MS Gg.4.27(1) IIIF bundle
rights.basis: The exact CUDL manifest says downloadable images are licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0; this file license is distinct from copyright in the published appendix drawings and from ownership of the physical manuscript.

rights.component: CUDL IIIF manifest and descriptive metadata for MS Gg.4.27(1)
rights.basis: The exact CUDL manifest separately licenses metadata under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0.

rights.component: Exact GND 117540579 JSON-LD authority metadata
rights.basis: The cached official record's described-by node assigns the metadata Creative Commons CC0 1.0.

rights.component: Official U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A PDF
rights.basis: Circular 15A is an official work of the United States Copyright Office prepared as part of federal governmental duties; 17 U.S.C. 105 makes United States copyright protection unavailable for United States Government works, subject to the statute's specified exceptions.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The Ellesmere manuscript's Canterbury Tales text plus related Chaucer textual materials from other witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: One-manuscript Ellesmere print with separately labeled appendices and illustration suites
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall keeps the main Ellesmere transcription distinct from Gamelyn and links sourced from Royal 18 C II, Arch. Selden B.14, and Dd.4.24, while adding Gg.4.27 drawings and Ellesmere illustration suites.
editorial_practices.scope: Entire edition, especially appendices pp. 1*-28* and plate matter

editorial_practices.representation: Printed as p. 275* but explicitly marked original perhaps and rejected
editorial_practices.description: The edition exposes the passage as editorial apparatus without silently incorporating it into the counted core lineation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed text, display matter, illustrations, and 846 page images
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR and Level-1 structural encoding linked to page milestones
editorial_practices.description: The U-M header states that no OCR correction or content editing was performed. Running text is encoded, while front matter, Parson analysis, plate suites, and most back matter are represented by omission containers and page breaks rather than transcribed content.

editorial_practices.representation: 846 monotonically increasing PB milestones with printed page and folio milestones where available
editorial_practices.description: The XML's 846 PB elements have unique SEQ values in exact order 1-846. The core text occupies PB 30-715; appendix text PB 718-746; the omitted 46-plate range PB 747-841; and further omitted back matter PB 842-846.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/agz8232.xml.

evidence.summary: Exact repository source for the item identifier, 2006 U-M publication and OCR declaration, source-edition bibliography, current encoded content, omission boundaries, appendix witness labels, and 846 page-image references.

evidence.citation: XPath, element-count, NODE, page-sequence, and content-boundary audit of the pinned agz8232 XML.
evidence.locator: DIV1-DIV3, HEAD, LG, L, P, PB, MILESTONE, N, NODE, TYPE, REF, and SEQ values
evidence.summary: Counts fifteen DIV1, fifty-three DIV2, seventy-five DIV3, 784 LG, 18,309 L, 267 P, 846 PB, and 1,017 MILESTONE elements; maps the ten core groups, appendices, omitted matter, exact page sequence, prose components, and the 75-part semantic inventory.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at pinned submodule commit.
evidence.locator: Complete 1,211-byte file; blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657
evidence.summary: The Unlicense text repeatedly scopes itself to software and source/binary code. It does not expressly license corpus XML, OCR, editorial data, images, or source editions.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, AGZ8232.0001.001, The Ellesmere ms. of Chaucer's Canterbury tales.
evidence.summary: Current item record for the title, creators, edition, public text and page-image routes, and public-domain/No Copyright-United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Digital Library, mssEL 26 C 9, The Ellesmere Chaucer.
evidence.summary: Official item record for shelfmark, c. 1400-1410 date, English origin, Middle English language, physical extent, twenty-two pilgrim miniatures plus likely Chaucer portrait, conservation, and digital-surrogate context.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, IIIF Presentation 2 manifest for p15150coll7:2838.
evidence.locator: 480 canvases; canvases 1-8, 9-472, and 473-480; manifest metadata and rights link
evidence.summary: Official 480-canvas inventory, labels, image services, physical metadata, scan-generation note, and pointer to Huntington rights policy.

evidence.citation: Deep inventory and image-integrity audit of the local Huntington 1600-pixel IIIF ZIP bundle.
evidence.locator: All 482 ZIP members and inventory records; first/last main-folio labels and image dimensions
evidence.summary: Verifies ZIP integrity, every recorded checksum and dimension, 1600-pixel image width, exact provider inventory coverage, and the f.1r/f.232v endpoints.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Library Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: States that Huntington does not require permission or charge fees to quote or publish public-domain material or material whose copyright it does not own, asks for source citation, and places responsibility for rights determination on the researcher.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and file inventory for ellesmeremschau00chaugoog.
evidence.summary: Fixes the item identifier, bibliographic metadata, 667-image carrier context, direct file names, and exact file byte counts for the PDFs, OCR, MARC, and scandata derivatives.

evidence.citation: Page-by-page text, page-object, and plate audit of the cached Internet Archive original and text PDFs for ellesmeremschau00chaugoog.
evidence.locator: PDF pages 1-667; especially p. 20, pp. 202-228 even, pp. 294-310 even, the Second Nun endpoint, Gamelyn, and terminal publisher matter
evidence.summary: Establishes the discontiguous partial coverage, exact main-text endpoint at Second Nun line 413, presence of Gamelyn pp. 1*-26*, all twenty-three Hooper cuts in two runs, Flügel correction leaf, and absence of later primary text, appendix pp. 27*-28*, Gg drawings, and miniature-facsimile suite.

evidence.citation: Page-object and normalized-text audit of the Internet Archive DjVu XML/plain-text derivatives for ellesmeremschau00chaugoog.
evidence.locator: 667 page objects; headings, line endpoints, low-word-count plate pages, and appendix transition
evidence.summary: Corroborates the PDF's textual endpoint and appendix transition and supports the plate audit; the cached plain text has 1,185,476 bytes.

evidence.citation: Google Books usage notice embedded at the beginning of the Internet Archive original PDF.
evidence.locator: PDF page 1
evidence.summary: Says Google believes the underlying book is public domain in the United States while requesting attribution and restricting use of the file to personal, non-commercial purposes; these are treated as scan-service terms, not copyright in the historical text.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons file page, The Ellesmere MS. of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, Part I (IA dli.granth.106881).pdf.
evidence.summary: Public file page and public-domain statement for the exact Commons-served PDF derivative.

evidence.citation: Deep structural and visual audit of the cached Wikimedia Commons DLI Granth PDF.
evidence.locator: All 782 pages; main text endpoints, appendix transitions, Parson analysis, correction and plate searches
evidence.summary: Confirms the full main printed text through Parson and Retraction, Gamelyn, rejected Clerk link, twenty-three Hooper cuts, and exact omitted apparatus: pp. 27*-28*, Gg drawings, miniature facsimiles, and Flügel correction.

evidence.summary: Documents the 1868-1879 edition, eight-part issue, forty-six plates, 1907 correction, reprint, and multiple full-view whole/part access objects without proving completeness of each individual provider object.

evidence.citation: The Online Books Page, Frederick James Furnivall author index.
evidence.summary: Indexes public HathiTrust page-image routes for multiple Ellesmere edition records and parts.

evidence.summary: Official shelfmark, catalog description, physical request route, folio context including Gamelyn at fols. 56v-67v, and notice that digital images are unavailable; catalog text is licensed CC BY.

evidence.summary: Verifies ZIP integrity, every inventory-recorded image checksum, complete 377-canvas provider coverage, and the target passage locator fol. 195v (Furnivall fol. 195b), canvas 348.

evidence.summary: Verifies ZIP integrity, every inventory-recorded image checksum, complete 1,144-canvas provider coverage, and exact labels at all nine loci for Furnivall's twelve appendix figures.

evidence.summary: Contextual catalog evidence for the separately printed Gg.4.27 witness representation; it is not treated as a complete manuscript facsimile.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Nationalbibliothek, GND 117540579, Furnivall, Frederick James.
evidence.locator: Preferred name; birth 1825-02-04; death 1910-07-02; differentiated-person type; CC0 metadata license
evidence.summary: Official authority evidence for the editor's identity and exact life dates. Name, dates, occupation, and the edition title-page attribution reconcile the intended Frederick James Furnivall despite differing legacy VIAF identifiers.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: Complete official PDF; April 2026 revision
evidence.summary: Supports the 2026 rule that works published in the United States before January 1, 1931 are in the public domain.

evidence.citation: United States Code, title 17, section 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Official statutory text stating that United States copyright protection is unavailable for works of the United States Government, subject to the section's specified exceptions.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of Copyright (term guidance).
evidence.summary: Supports life-plus-70 literary-work duration, 25-year typographical-arrangement duration, and the need to distinguish published from historically unpublished works. This is mutable official HTML rather than a versioned file, so the access date is material and no local checksum is asserted.

open_questions.question: How should the current U-M No Copyright-United States statement interact with the embedded 2006 copyright/restrictions notice for redistribution of OCR and markup?
next_steps: Treat the current item statement as the provider's present designation.
Preserve the older embedded notice and avoid inferring that the CME software Unlicense covers XML/data.
Seek written U-M clarification before high-risk redistribution of the encoding as a standalone dataset.

open_questions.question: Does U-M expose a documented bulk download for the exact 846 page images, and what item-specific terms attach to that route?
next_steps: Use the public viewer and stable PB inventory for current access claims.
Do not describe a bulk image download until an exact documented endpoint and terms page are verified.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current Bodleian catalog record and digital-access status for MS Arch. Selden B.14?
next_steps: Retain Furnivall's shelfmark-level source claim at medium confidence.
Search the current Bodleian Archives and Manuscripts catalogue by shelfmark before any witness-level image request.

open_questions.question: Which individual HathiTrust object, if any, reproduces every part, all forty-six plates, appendix pp. 27*-28*, and the 1907 correction leaf in one sequence?
next_steps: Keep HathiTrust records as access routes rather than audited complete carriers.
Audit a specific volume object before assigning complete local-copy coverage.
